channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Context Management
channel.description: Latest Context Management news in Context Engineering

item.title: Support Sufficiency as Consequence-Sensitive Compression in Belief Arbitration
item.description: arXiv:2604.16434v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When a system commits to a hypothesis, much of the evidential structure behind that commitment is lost to compression. Standard accounts assume that selected content and scalar confidence suffice for downstream control. This paper argues that they do ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, paper, RAG, arxiv, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BASIS: Balanced Activation Sketching with Invariant Scalars for "Ghost Backpropagation"
item.description: arXiv:2604.16324v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The activation memory required for exact backpropagation scales linearly with network depth, context length, and feature dimensionality, forming an O(L * BN ) spatial bottleneck (where B is the sequence-batch cardinality and N is the feature dimension...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, paper, arxiv, model, compression | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OpenViking - OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.
item.description: OpenViking is an open-source context database designed specifically for AI Agents(such as openclaw). OpenViking unifies the management of context (memory, resources, and skills) that Agents need through a file system paradigm, enabling hierarchical context delivery and self-evolving.<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> GitHub | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (GitHub)
